author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXXV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …The title of it causeth us no delay, for it is both brief, and to be understood not difficult, especially to those nursed in the Church of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so it is, “To David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm then is to David himself: now David is interpreted, Strong in hand, or Desirable.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalm then is to the Strong in hand, and Desirable, to Him who for us hath overcome death, who unto us hath promised life: for in this is He Strong in hand, that He hath overcome death for us; in this is He Desirable, that He hath promised unto us life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what stronger than that Hand which touched the bier, and he that was dead rose up?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What stronger than that Hand which overcame the world, not armed with steel, but pierced with wood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or what more desirable than He, whom not having seen, the Martyrs wished even to die, that they might be worthy to come unto Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore is the Psalm unto Him: to Him let our heart, to Him our tongue sing worthily: if yet Himself shall deign to give somewhat to sing.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Judge Thou, O Lord” (saith he), “them that hurt me, and fight Thou against them that fight against me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If God be for us, who can be against us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whereby doth God this for us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Take hold” (saith he) “of arms and shield, and rise up to my help” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A great spectacle is it, to see God armed for thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is His Shield, what are His Arms?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord,” in another place saith the man who here also speaketh, “as with the shield of Thy good-will hast Thou compassed us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But His Arms, wherewith He may not only us defend, but also strike His enemies, if we have well profited, shall we ourselves be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as we from Him have this, that we be armed, so is He armed from us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He is armed from those whom He hath made, we are armed with those things which we have received from Him who made us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These our arms the Apostle in a certain place calleth, “The shield of Faith, the helmet of Salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the Word of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath armed us with such arms as ye have heard, arms admirable, and unconquered, insuperable and shining; spiritual truly and invisible, because we have to fight also against invisible enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou seest thine enemy, let thine arms be seen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are armed with faith in those things which we see not, and we overthrow enemies whom we see not.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Pour forth the weapon, and stop the way against them that persecute me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that persecute thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Haply thy neighbour, or he whom thou hast offended, or to whom thou hast done wrong, or who would take away what is thine, or against whom thou preachest the truth, or whose sin thou rebukest, or whom living ill by thy well living thou offendest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are indeed even these enemies to us, and they persecute us: but other enemies we are taught to know, those against whom we fight invisibly, of whom the Apostle warneth us, saying, “We wrestle not against flesh and blood,” that is, against men; not against those whom ye see, but against those whom ye see not; “against principalities, against powers, against the rulers of the world, of this darkness.”…“The whole world lieth in wickedness;” therefore the Apostle explained of what world they were rulers, he said, “of this darkness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rulers of this world, I say, are the rulers of this darkness.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be confounded and put to shame, that seek after my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4): for to this end they seek after it, to destroy it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I would that they would seek it for good! for in another Psalm he blameth this in men, that there was none who would seek after his soul: “Refuge failed me: there was none that would seek after my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is this that saith, “There was none that would seek after my soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it haply He, of whom so long before it was predicted, “They pierced My Hands and My Feet, they numbered all My Bones, they stared and looked upon Me, they have parted My Garments among them, and cast lots for My Vesture”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now all these things were done before their eyes, and there was none who would seek after His Soul.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5. …Many have been confounded to their health: many, put to shame, have passed over from the persecution of Christ to the society of His members with devoted piety; and this would not have been, had they not been confounded and put to shame.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore he wished well to them.…Let them not go before, but follow; let them not give counsel, but take it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Peter would go before the Lord, when the Lord spake of His future Passion: he would to Him as it were give counsel for His health.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sick man to the Saviour give counsel for His health!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what said he to the Lord, affirming that His future Passion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be it far from Thee, Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be gracious to Thyself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This shall not be to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would go before that the Lord might follow; and what said He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Get thee behind Me, Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By going before thou art Satan, by following thou wilt be a disciple.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same then is said to these also, “Let them be turned back and brought to confusion that think evil against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when they have begun to follow after, now they will not think evil against me, but desire my good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What of others?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all are not so conquered as to be converted and believe: many continue in obstinacy, many preserve in heart the spirit of going before, and if they exert it not, yet they labour with it, and finding opportunity bring it forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of such, what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be as dust before the wind” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Not so are the ungodly, not so; but as the dust which the wind driveth away from the face of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wind is temptation; the dust are the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When temptation cometh, the dust is raised, it neither standeth nor resisteth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be as dust before the wind, and let the Angel of the Lord trouble them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let their way be darkness and slipping” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A horrible way!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Darkness alone who feareth not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A slippery way alone who avoids not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a dark and slippery way how shalt thou go? where set foot?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These two ills are the great punishments of men: darkness, ignorance; a slippery way, luxury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let the Angel of the Lord persecute them;” that they be not able to stand.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For any one in a dark and slippery way, when he seeth that if he move his foot he will fall, and there is no light before his feet, haply resolveth to wait until light come; but here is the Angel of the Lord persecuting them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These things he predicted would come upon them, not as though he wished them to happen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although the Prophet in the Spirit of God so speaketh these things, even as God doth the same, with sure judgment, with a judgment good, righteous, holy, tranquil; not moved with wrath, not with bitter jealousy, not with desire of wreaking enmities, but of punishing wickedness with righteousness; nevertheless, it is a prophecy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore these so great evils?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By what desert?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear by what desert.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For without cause have they hid for me the corruption of their trap” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Him that is our Head, observe, the Jews did this: they hid the corruption of their trap.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whom hid they their trap?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For Him who saw the hearts of those that hid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet was He among them like one ignorant, as though He were deceived, whereas they were in that deceived, that they thought Him to be deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For therefore was He as though deceived, living among them, because we among such as they were so to live, as to be without doubt deceived.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saw His betrayer, and chose him the more to a necessary work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By his evil He wrought a great good: and yet among the twelve was he chosen, lest even the small number of twelve should be without one evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was an example of patience to us, because it was necessary that we should live among the evil: it was necessary that we should endure the evil, either knowing them or knowing them not: an example of patience He gave thee lest thou shouldest fail, when thou hast begun to live among the evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because that School of Christ in the twelve failed not, how much more ought we to be firm, when in the great Church is fulfilled what was predicted of the mixture of the evil.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But yet what is to be done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Without a cause have they hid for me the corruption of their trap.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “Without a cause”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have done them no evil, I have hurt them not at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Vainly have they reviled my soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Vainly”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Speaking falsely, proving nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let a trap come upon them which they know not of” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A magnificent retribution, nothing more just!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have hidden a trap that I might know not: let a trap come upon them which they know not of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I know of their trap.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what trap is coming upon them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which they know not of.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear, lest haply he speak of that.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let a trap come upon them, which they know not of.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps that is one which they hid for him, that another which shall come upon themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so: but what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The wicked shall be holden with the cords of his own sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thereby are they deceived, whereby they would deceive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence shall come mischief to them, whence they endeavoured mischief.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it follows, “And let the net which they have hidden catch themselves, and let them fall into their own trap.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if any one should prepare a cup of poison for another, and forgetting should drink it up himself: or as if one should dig a pit, that his enemy might fall thereinto in the darkness and himself forgetting what he had dug, should first walk that way, and fall into it.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then for the wicked that would hurt me: what for me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But my soul shall rejoice in the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9); as in Him from whom it hath heard, “I am thy salvation;” as not seeking other riches from without; as not seeking to abound in pleasures and good things of earth; but loving freely the true Spouse, not from Him wishing to receive aught that may delight, but Him alone proposing to itself, by whom it may be delighted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what better than God will be given unto me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God loveth me: God loveth thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See He hath proposed to thee, Ask what thou wilt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the emperor should say to thee, Ask what thou wilt, what commands, what dignities, wouldest thou burst forth with!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What great things wouldest thou propose to thyself, both to receive and to bestow!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When God saith unto thee, Ask what thou wilt, what wilt thou ask? empty thy mind, exert thy avarice, stretch forward as far as possible, and enlarge thy desire: it is not any one, but Almighty God that said, Ask what thou wilt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If of possessions thou art a lover, thou wilt desire the whole earth, that all who are born may be thy husbandmen, or thy slaves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what when thou hast possessed the whole earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt ask the sea, in which yet thou canst not live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this greediness the fishes will have the better of thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But perhaps thou wilt possess the islands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pass over these also; ask the air although thou canst not fly; stretch thy desire even unto the heavens, call thine own the sun, the moon, and the stars, because He who made all said, Ask what thou wilt: yet nothing wilt thou find more precious, nothing wilt thou find better, than Himself who made all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Him seek, who made all things, and in Him and from Him shalt thou have all things which He made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things are precious, because all are beautiful; but what more beautiful than He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Strong are they; but what stronger than He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And nothing would He give thee rather than Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If aught better thou hast found, ask it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou ask aught else, thou wilt do wrong to Him, and harm to thyself, by preferring to Him that which He made, when He would give to thee Himself who made.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But my soul shall be joyful in the Lord; it shall rejoice in His salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The salvation of God is Christ: “For mine eyes have seen Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All my bones shall say, Lord, who is like unto Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can speak anything worthily of these words?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I think them only to be pronounced, not to be expounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why seekest thou this or that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is like unto thy Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Him hast thou before thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The unrighteous have declared unto me delights, but not after Thy law, O Lord!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Persecutors have been who have said, Worship Saturn, worship Mercury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I worship not idols (saith he): “Lord, who is like unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have eyes, and see not; ears have they, but they hear not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, who is like unto Thee,” who hast made the eye to see, the ear to hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I (saith he) worship not idols, for them a workman made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Worship a tree or mountain; did a workman make them also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here too, Lord, who is like unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Earthly things are shown unto me; Thou art Creator of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from these haply they turn to the higher creation, and say to me, Worship the Moon, worship this Sun, who with his light, as a great lamp in the Heavens, maketh the day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here also I plainly say, “Lord, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Moon and the Stars Thou hast made, the Sun to rule the day hast Thou kindled, the Heavens hast Thou framed together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are many invisible things better.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But haply here also it is said to me, Worship Angels, adore Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And here also will I say, “Lord, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even the Angels Thou hast created.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Angels are nothing, but by seeing Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is better with them to possess Thee, than by worshipping them to fall from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Body of Christ, Holy Church, let all thy bones say, “Lord, who is like unto thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if the flesh under persecution hath fallen away, let the bones say, “Lord, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of the righteous it is said, “The Lord keepeth all their bones; not one of them shall be broken.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of how many righteous have the bones under persecution been broken?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Finally, “The just shall live by faith,” and “Christ justifieth the ungodly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how justifieth He any except believing and confessing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness, and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore also that thief, although from His theft led to the judge, and from the judge to the cross, yet on the very cross was justified: with his heart he believed, with his mouth he confessed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For neither to a man unrighteous and not already justified, would the Lord have said, “To-day shalt thou be with Me in Paradise,” and yet his bones were broken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when they came to take down the bodies, by reason of the approaching Sabbath, the Lord was found already dead, and His Bones were not broken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But of those that yet lived, that they might be taken down, the legs were broken, that so from this pain having died, they might be buried.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were then of the one thief, who persisted in his ungodliness on the cross, the bones broken, and not also of the other who with his heart believed, and with his mouth made confession unto salvation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is that which was said, “The Lord keepeth all his bones; not one of them shall be broken;” except that in the Body of the Lord the name of bones is given to all the righteous, the firm in heart, the strong, yielding to no persecutions, no temptations, so as to consent unto evil?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Which deliverest the poor from him that is too strong for him; yea, the poor and needy from him that spoileth him.”…Who that deliverest, but He who is Strong in hand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even that David shall deliver the poor from him that is too strong for him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the devil was too strong for thee, and held thee, because he conquered thee, when thou consentedst unto him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what hath the Strong in hand done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “No man entereth into a strong man’s house, to spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By His own Power, most Holy, most Magnificent, hath He bound the devil by pouring forth the weapon to stop the way against him, that He may deliver the poor and needy, to whom there was no helper.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who is thy helper but the Lord to whom thou sayest, “O Lord, My Strength, and My Redeemer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wilt presume of thy own strength, thereby wilt thou fall, whereof thou hast presumed: if of another’s, he would lord it over thee, not succour thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then alone is to be sought Who hath redeemed them, and made them free, and hath given His Blood to purchase them, and of His servants hath made them His Brethren.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then our Head say, “False witnesses did rise up, they laid to My charge things that I knew not” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let us say to our Head, Lord, what knewest Thou not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst Thou indeed know not anything?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst Thou not know the hearts of them that charged Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst Thou not foresee their deceits?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst Thou not give Thyself into their hands knowingly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hadst Thou not come that Thou mightest suffer by them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then knewest Thou not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He knew not sin, and thereby He knew not sin, not by not judging, but by not committing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are phrases of this kind also in daily use, as when thou sayest of any one, He knoweth not to stand, that is, he doth not stand; and, He knoweth not to do good, because he doth not good; and, He knoweth not to do ill, because he doth not ill.…What knew not Christ so much, as to blaspheme?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thereof was He called in question by His persecutors, and because He spake truth, He was judged to have spoken blasphemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But by whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By them of whom it followeth, “They rewarded Me evil for good, and barrenness to My Soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I gave unto them fruitfulness, they rewarded Me barrenness; I gave life, they death; I honour, they dishonour; I medicine, they wounds; and in all these which they rewarded Me, was truly barrenness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This barrenness in the tree He cursed, when seeking fruit He found none.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Leaves there were, and fruit there was not: words there were, and deeds there were not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See of words abundance, and of deeds barrenness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou that preachest a man should not steal, stealest: thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery, committest adultery.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such were they who charged Christ with things that He knew not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I, when they troubled me, clothed myself with sackcloth, and humbled my soul with fasting, and my prayer shall return into mine own bosom” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 13)…Brethren, if for some little space with pious curiosity we lift the veil, and search with the intent eye of the heart the inner part of this Scripture, we find that even this the Lord did.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sackcloth, haply He calleth His mortal flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore Sackcloth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the likeness of sinful flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostle saith, “God sent His Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, that through sin He might condemn sin in the flesh:” that is, He clothed His Own Son with sackcloth, that through sackcloth He might condemn the goats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not that there was sin, I say not in the Word of God, but not even in that Holy Soul and Mind of a Man, which the Word and Wisdom of God had so joined to Himself as to be One Person.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, nor even in His very Body was any sin, but the likeness of sinful flesh there was in the Lord; because death is not but by sin, and surely that Body was mortal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had It not been mortal, It had not died; had It not died, It had not risen again; had It not risen again, It had not showed us an example of eternal life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then death, which is caused by sin, is called sin; as we say the Greek tongue, the Latin tongue, meaning not the very member of flesh, but that which is done by the member of flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the tongue in our members is one among others, as the eyes, nose, ears, and the rest: but the Greek tongue is Greek words, not that the tongue is words, but that words are by the tongue.…So then the sin of the Lord is that which was caused by sin; because He assumed flesh, of the same lump which had deserved death by sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to speak more briefly, Mary who was of Adam died for sin, Adam died for sin, and the Flesh of the Lord which was of Mary died to put away sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With this sackcloth the Lord clothed Himself, and therefore was He not known, because He lay hid under sackcloth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When they,” saith He, “troubled Me, I clothed Myself with sackcloth:” that is, they raged, I lay hid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had He not willed to lie hid neither could He have died, since in one moment of time one drop only of His Power, if indeed it is to be called a drop, He put forth, when they wished to seize Him, and at His one question, “Whom seek ye?” they all went back and fell to the ground.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such power could He not have humbled in passion, if He had not lain hid under sackcloth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, if we have understood the sackcloth, how understand we the fasting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wished Christ to eat, when He sought fruit on the tree, and if He had found, would He have eaten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wished Christ to drink, when He said to the woman of Samaria, “Give Me to drink”? when He said on the Cross, “I thirst”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what hungered, for what thirsted Christ, but our good works?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in them that crucified and persecuted Him He had found no good works, He fasted; for they rewarded barrenness to His soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what a fast was His, who found barely one thief, whom on the Cross He might taste!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Apostles had fled, and had hidden themselves in the multitude.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And even Peter, who even to the death of his Lord had promised to persevere, had now thrice denied Him, had now wept, and still lay hid in the multitude, still feared lest He should be known.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, having seen Him dead, all of them despaired of their own safety and despairing He found them, after His resurrection, and when He spake with them, found them grieving and mourning, no longer hoping anything.…In great fasting had the Lord remained, had He not refreshed them that He might feed on them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He refreshed them, He comforted them, He confirmed them, and into His Own Body converted them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this manner then was our Lord also in fasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My prayer shall return into Mine Own Bosom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the bosom of this verse is plainly a great depth, and may the Lord grant that it be fathomable by us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the “bosom” a secret is understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And we ourselves, Brethren, are here well admonished to pray within our own bosom, where God seeth, where God heareth, where no human eye penetrateth, where none seeth but He who succoureth; where Susanna prayed, and her voice, though it was not heard by men, yet by God was heard.…We read also that in the mount Jesus prayed alone, we read that He passed the night in prayer, even at the time of His Passion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My prayer shall return into Mine Own Bosom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I know not what better to understand concerning the Lord: take meanwhile what now occurs; perhaps something better will occur hereafter, either to me or to some better: “My prayer shall return into Mine Own Bosom:” this I understand to be said, because in His Own Bosom He had the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God was in Christ reconciling the world unto Himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Himself He had Him to whom He prayed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was not far from Him, for Himself had said, “I am in the Father, and the Father in Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because prayer rather belongeth to very Man (for according as Christ is the Word, He prayeth not, but heareth prayer; and seeketh not to be succoured for Himself, but with the Father succoureth all): what is, “My prayer shall return into Mine Own Bosom,” but in Me My Manhood invoketh in Me My Godhead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As a Neighbour, as our Brother, so I pleased Him: as one mourning and sorrowful, so I humbled myself” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now looketh He back to His Own Body: let us now look to this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we rejoice in prayer, when our mind is calmed, not by the world’s prosperity, but by the light of Truth: (who perceiveth this light, knoweth what I say, and he seeth and acknowledgeth what is said, “As a Neighbour, as our Brother, so I pleased Him”): even then our soul pleaseth God, not placed afar off, for, “In Him,” saith one, “we live and move and have our being,” but as a Brother, as a Neighbour, as a Friend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if it be not such that it can so rejoice, so shine, so approach, so cleave unto Him, and seeth itself far off thence, then let it do what followeth, “As one mourning and sorrowful, so I humbled Myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As our Brother, so I pleased Him,” said He, drawing near; “As one mourning and sorrowful, so I humbled Myself,” said He, removed and set afar off.…Did not Peter draw near, when he said, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the Living God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet the same man became afar off by saying, “Be it far from Thee, Lord; this shall not be unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, what said He, his Neighbour, as it were, to him drawing near?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed art thou, Simon, Barjona.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To him afar off, as it were, and unlike, what said He?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Get thee behind Me, Satan.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To him drawing near, “Flesh and blood,” saith He, “hath not revealed it unto thee, but My Father, which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Light is shed over thee, in His Light thou shinest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when having become afar off, he spake against the Lord’s Passion, which should be for our Salvation, “Thou savourest not,” said He, “the things that be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One rightly placing together both of these saith in a certain Psalm, “I said in my ecstasy, I am cast off from before Thine Eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In my ecstasy, would he not have said, had he not drawn near; for ecstasy is the transporting of the mind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He poured over himself his own soul, and drew near unto God; and through some cloud and weight of the flesh being again cast down to earth, and recollecting where he had been, and seeing where he was, he said, “I am cast off from before Thine Eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then, “As a Neighbour, as our Brother, so I pleased Him,” may He grant to be done in us; but when that is not, let even this be done, “As one mourning and sorrowful, so I humbled myself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And against Me they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 15), against Me only: they rejoicing, I sorrowful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we heard just now in the Gospel, “Blessed are they that mourn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they are blessed that mourn, miserable are they that laugh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Against Me they rejoiced, and gathered themselves together: scourges were gathered together against Me, and they knew not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because they laid to My charge things that I knew not, they also knew not Whom they charged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They tempted Me, and mocked Me with mocking” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, they derided Me, they insulted Me; this of the Head, this of the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider, Brethren, the glory of the Church which now is; remember its past dishonours, remember how once were Christians everywhere put to flight, and wherever found, mocked, beaten, slain, exposed to beasts, burned, men rejoicing against them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it was to the Head, so it is also to the Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as it was to the Lord on the Cross, so has it been to His Body in all that persecution which was made but now: nor even now cease the persecutions of the same.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherever men find a Christian, they are wont to insult, to persecute, to deride him, to call him dull, senseless, of no spirit, of no knowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do they what they will, Christ is in Heaven: do they what they will, He hath honoured His punishment, already hath He fixed His Cross in the foreheads of all; the ungodly is permitted to insult, to rage he is not permitted; but yet from that which the tongue uttereth, is understood what he beareth in his heart: “They gnashed upon Me with their teeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, when wilt Thou look on?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rescue My Soul from their deceits, My Darling from the lions” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to us the time is slow; and in our person is this said, “When wilt Thou look on?” that is, when shall we see vengeance upon those who insult us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall the Judge, overcome by weariness, hear the widow?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But our Judge, not from weariness, but from love, delayeth our salvation; from reason, not from need; not that He could not even now succour us, but that the number of us all may be filled up even to the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet out of our desire, what do we say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, when wilt Thou look on?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rescue My Soul from their deceits, My Darling from the lions:” that is, My Church from raging powers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, wouldest thou know what is that Darling?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Read the words following: “I will confess unto Thee, O Lord, in the great Congregation; in a weighty people will I praise Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly saith He, “I will confess unto Thee:” for confession is made in all the multitude, but not in all is God praised: the whole multitude heareth our confession, but not in all the multitude is the praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in all the whole multitude, that is, in the Church which is spread abroad in the whole world, is chaff, and wheat: the chaff flieth, the wheat remaineth; therefore, “in a weighty people will I praise Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In a weighty people, which the wind of temptation carries not away, in such is God praised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the chaff He is ever blasphemed.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let not them that are Mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over Me:” for they rejoice over Me because of My chaff.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hate Me without a cause;” that is, whom I never hurt; “winking with their eyes” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 19): that is, pretending hypocrites, “For they spake indeed peace to Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “winking with their eyes”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Declaring by their looks, what they carry not in their heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who are these “winking with their eyes”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they spake indeed peace to Me; and with wrath devised craftily.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea they opened their mouth wide against Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First winking with their eyes, those lions sought to ravish and devour; first fawning they spake peace, and then with wrath devised craftily.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What peace spake they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Master, we know that Thou acceptest not man’s person, and teachest the way of God in truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar, or not?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They spake indeed peace unto Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Didst not Thou know them, and deceived they Thee, winking with their eyes?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly He knew them; therefore said He, “Why tempt ye Me, ye hypocrites?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Afterward, “they opened their mouth wide against Me,” crying, “Crucify Him, Crucify Him! and said, Aha, Aha, our eyes have seen it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This, when they insulted Him, “Aha, Aha, Prophesy unto us, Thou Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As their peace was pretended when they tempted Him concerning the money, so now insulting was their praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They said, Aha, Aha, our eyes have seen it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 21): that is, Thy deeds, Thy miracles.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Man is the Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If He be the Christ, let Him come down from the Cross, and we will believe Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saved others, Himself He cannot save.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our eyes have seen it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is all whereof He boasted Himself, when “He called Himself the Son of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the Lord was hanging patient upon the Cross: His power had He not lost, but He showed His patience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what great thing was it for Him to come down from the Cross, who could afterward rise again from the sepulchre?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He seems to have yielded to His insulters; and this, beloved, that having risen again He should show Himself to His own, and not to them, and this is a great mystery; for His resurrection signified the New Life, but the New Life is known to His friends, not to His enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This Thou hast seen, O Lord; keep not silence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “keep not silence”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of judgment is it said in a certain place, “I have kept silence; shall I keep silence for ever?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And of the delaying of judgment it is said to the sinner, “These things hast thou done, and I kept silence;” “Thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How keepeth He silence, who speaketh by the Prophets, who speaketh with His own mouth in the Gospel, who speaketh by the Evangelists, who speaketh by us, when we speak the truth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He keepeth silence from judgment, not from precept, not from doctrine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this His judgment the Prophet in a manner invoketh, and predicteth: “Thou hast seen, O Lord: keep not silence;” that is, Thou wilt not keep silence, needs must that Thou wilt judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, be not far from Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Until Thy judgment come, be not far from Me, as Thou hast promised, “Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Arise, Lord, and attend to My judgment” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Thou art in tribulation; that Thou art tormented with labours and pains?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not even many wicked men suffer the same?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To what judgment?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore art Thou righteous, because Thou sufferest these things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: but what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To My judgment.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Attend to My judgment; even to My cause, My God, and My Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to My punishment, but to My cause: not to that which the robber hath in common with Me, but to that whereof is said, “Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this cause is distinguished.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For punishment is equal to good and bad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore Martyrs, not the punishment, but the cause maketh, for if punishment made Martyrs, all the mines would be full of Martyrs, every chain would drag Martyrs, all that are executed with the sword would be crowned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore let the cause be distinguished; let none say, because I suffer, I am righteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He who first suffered, suffered for righteousness’ sake, therefore He added a great exception, “Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness’ sake.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many having a good cause do persecution, and many having a bad cause suffer persecution.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if persecution could not be done rightly, it had not been said in a certain Psalm, “Whoso privily slandereth his neighbour, him did I persecute.”…Let none then say, I suffer persecution: let him not sift the punishment, but prove the cause: lest if he prove not the cause, he be numbered with the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore how watchfully, how excellently hath This Man recommended Himself, “O Lord, attend to My judgment,” not to My punishments; “even to My cause, My God, and My Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Judge me, O Lord, according to My righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 24); that is, attend to My cause.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not according to My punishment, but “according to My righteousness, O Lord, My God,” that is, according to this judge Thou Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And let them not rejoice over Me;” that is, Mine enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not say in their heart, Aha, aha, so would we have it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 25); that is, We have done what we could, we have slain him, we have taken him away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not say:” show them that they have done nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not say, We have swallowed him up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence say those Martyrs, “If the Lord had not been on our side, then they had swallowed us up quick.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “had swallowed us up”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Had passed into their own body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that thou swallowest up, which thou passest into thy own body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The world would swallow thee up; swallow thou the world, pass it into thy own body: kill and eat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it was said to Peter, “Kill and eat;” do thou kill in them what they are, make them what thou art.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if they on the other hand persuade thee to ungodliness, thou art swallowed up by them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not when they persecute thee art thou swallowed up by them, but when they persuade thee to be what they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them not say, We have swallowed him up.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou swallow up the body of Pagans.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why the body of Pagans?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It would swallow thee up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou to it, what it would to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore perhaps that calf, being ground to powder, was cast into the water and given to the children of Israel to drink, that so the body of ungodliness might be swallowed up by Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 26); so that we may swallow up them ashamed and brought to confusion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who speak evil against me:” let them be ashamed, let them be brought to confusion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sayest thou now, the Head with the Members?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them shout for joy and be glad that favour My righteous cause:” who cleave to My Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, let them say “continually, Let the Lord be magnified, which hath pleasure in the prosperity of His servant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And my tongue shall speak of Thy righteousness, and of Thy praise all the day long” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whose tongue endureth to speak the praise of God all the day long?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See now I have made a discourse something longer; ye are wearied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who endureth to praise God all the day long?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will suggest a remedy, whereby thou mayest praise God all the day long if thou wilt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever thou dost, do well, and thou hast praised God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou singest an hymn, thou praisest God, but what doth thy tongue, unless thy heart also praise Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hast thou ceased from singing hymns, and departed, that thou mayest refresh thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not drunken, and thou hast praised God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou go away to sleep?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rise not to do evil, and thou hast praised God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou transact business?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do no wrong, and thou hast praised God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou till thy field?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Raise not strife, and thou hast praised God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the innocency of thy works prepare thyself to praise God all the day long.
